```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A global logistics platform must display real-time earnings breakdowns to drivers across three jurisdictions with fundamentally different labor classifications, legal requirements, and approval processes. The feature intersects employment law, compensation transparency regulation, and works council governance — making it a compliance-critical initiative, not merely a product feature.",
    "bounded_context": "Driver Compensation & Earnings Visibility",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Base Pay", "definition": "The guaranteed compensation a driver receives for a work period or delivery, independent of tips or bonuses. For employees (CA), this must meet or exceed minimum wage. For contractors (Indonesia), this is the agreed service fee." },
      { "term": "Per-Delivery Bonus", "definition": "Variable compensation earned for each completed delivery, calculated on top of base pay." },
      { "term": "Tip", "definition": "Voluntary gratuity from the customer directed to the driver. Must never be commingled with or subsidize base pay — especially in employee jurisdictions." },
      { "term": "Deduction", "definition": "Any amount subtracted from gross earnings — including platform fees, equipment costs, tax withholdings, or insurance. Each deduction must be individually itemized with a reason code." },
      { "term": "Gross Earnings", "definition": "Sum of base pay + per-delivery bonuses + tips, before any deductions." },
      { "term": "Net Earnings", "definition": "Gross earnings minus all deductions. The amount the driver actually receives." },
      { "term": "Earnings Breakdown", "definition": "The structured, itemized view of all compensation components and deductions for a given period." },
      { "term": "Driver Classification", "definition": "The legal labor status of the driver in a given jurisdiction: employee or independent contractor. Determines which wage laws, deduction rules, and transparency obligations apply." },
      { "term": "Works Council (Betriebsrat)", "definition": "German employee representative body with codetermination rights. Must formally approve changes to compensation display systems before deployment in Germany." },
      { "term": "Earnings Period", "definition": "The time window over which an earnings breakdown is calculated — a single delivery, a shift, a day, a pay period." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Every earnings breakdown must individually itemize base pay, per-delivery bonuses, tips, and each deduction as separate line items. No component may be aggregated into an opaque total.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Commingling or hiding earnings components corrupts the data integrity of the compensation record and violates the core purpose of the feature.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given any earnings breakdown, ASSERT that base_pay, bonuses, tips, and deductions are each present as individually queryable fields, and that gross_earnings == base_pay + sum(bonuses) + tips, and net_earnings == gross_earnings - sum(deductions)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Tips must never subsidize or reduce base pay. A driver's base pay must be calculated independently of tips received.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Tip credit against base pay is illegal for employees in California and is a fundamental business integrity rule. Even in contractor jurisdictions, allowing tip subsidization would be a reputational and legal risk.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with tip amount T, ASSERT that base_pay is identical to what it would be if T were zero."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "In California, drivers are classified as employees under AB5. The earnings breakdown must display gross pay, all itemized deductions (taxes, benefits, etc.), and net pay — consistent with California wage statement requirements (Labor Code §226).",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "California law mandates specific wage statement content. Non-compliance exposes the platform to statutory penalties per pay period per employee. This is a legal constraint, not a policy choice.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a California driver's earnings breakdown, ASSERT it contains: gross wages, total hours worked, all itemized deductions with labels, net wages, pay period dates, and employer identification."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "In California, the displayed base pay for any earnings period must reflect at least the applicable minimum wage multiplied by hours worked.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Minimum wage is a legal floor for employees. Displaying earnings below this threshold means either the calculation or the pay itself is unlawful.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a California driver's earnings breakdown with H hours worked, ASSERT that base_pay >= california_minimum_wage * H."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "The Germany earnings display feature must not be deployed to German drivers until formal works council (Betriebsrat) approval is obtained and recorded.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "German codetermination law (BetrVG §87) gives works councils mandatory co-decision rights on monitoring and compensation systems. Deploying without approval is a legal violation that can result in injunctions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given the Germany jurisdiction, ASSERT that the feature flag for earnings display is gated behind a works_council_approval_status == 'approved' check, and that no German driver can access the feature when this status is 'pending' or 'not_requested'."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Each deduction must have a reason code and human-readable description. No deduction may be applied or displayed without both.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "An unexplained deduction corrupts the transparency guarantee and, in employee jurisdictions, violates wage statement requirements.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given any deduction in an earnings breakdown, ASSERT that deduction.reason_code is non-null AND deduction.description is a non-empty string."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Earnings data must be displayed in the local currency of the jurisdiction where the driver operates.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying earnings in a foreign currency misrepresents what the driver actually receives and creates confusion that undermines the transparency purpose.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver in jurisdiction J, ASSERT that all monetary values in the earnings breakdown use J's local currency code (USD for California, EUR for Germany, IDR for Indonesia)."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "The earnings breakdown must be available in near-real-time — updated within 5 minutes of a delivery completion event.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 5-minute SLA is a product policy target, not a legal or data-integrity constraint. It could be relaxed to 15 minutes or tightened to 1 minute based on business needs.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a delivery completed at time T, ASSERT that the driver's earnings breakdown reflects that delivery's compensation by T + 5 minutes."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "In Indonesia, independent contractor drivers must see a clear indication that displayed amounts are gross service fees and that tax obligations are the driver's responsibility.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a best-practice disclosure for contractor relationships, not a hard legal mandate in Indonesia. However, omitting it creates misunderstanding and support burden.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an Indonesian driver's earnings breakdown, ASSERT that a tax_responsibility_disclaimer is present and visible."
    },
    {

      "rule": "Deductions permitted for employees (CA, Germany) must be limited to those authorized by law or explicitly consented to by the driver. No novel deduction category may be introduced without legal review.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Unauthorized deductions from employee wages are illegal in both California and Germany. This is a hard legal constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given any deduction applied to an employee driver, ASSERT that deduction.reason_code exists in the jurisdiction's authorized_deduction_types list."
    }

      "then": "gross_earnings == base_pay + sum(bonuses) + tips AND net_earnings == gross_earnings - sum(deductions) — with zero tolerance for rounding drift beyond the currency's smallest unit"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A driver completes a delivery that crosses a pay period boundary (e.g., started before midnight, completed after)", "expected_behavior": "The delivery earnings are attributed to the pay period in which the delivery was completed, not started.     { "scenario": "A customer adds or modifies a tip after the initial earnings breakdown was displayed", "expected_behavior": "The earnings breakdown updates to reflect the new tip amount with an audit trail showing the adjustment. Base pay must not change as a result." },
```
